scope: Candidate Maasai text, speech, media, and application leads tracked for rights-aware discovery, review, and possible future ingest.

status_definitions.approved: Rights basis is documented and the item may enter a controlled ingest lane.
status_definitions.gated_access: The item appears useful, but access terms, permission, or explicit approval must be completed first.
status_definitions.reference_only: The item may inform research, evaluation, or architecture work, but not source ingest.

automation_policy.notes: Openly reachable content is not automatically ingestible. Human voice, minors, and culturally sensitive material always require manual review.

candidates.title: Vocabulary of the enguduk iloigob, as spoken by the Masai-tribes in East-Africa

rights.rights_notes: Published in 1857. Commons identifies the Internet Archive scan and marks the work as public domain in the United States.
rights.restrictions: Treat as historical Maa/Iloikop material with explicit dialect-era labeling and page-level provenance.

sensitivity.notes: Historical orthography and colonial framing require review before mixing with modern datasets.

candidates.next_action: Download the PDF, run manual page triage, and extract only high-confidence vocabulary pairs with source-page citations.

candidates.title: Maasai Dictionary

rights.rights_notes: The press policy says publications are hosted under a Creative Commons license chosen by the author, but the Maasai Dictionary item page does not identify which license applies to this work.
rights.restrictions: Do not ingest until the exact reuse grant is documented in the PDF colophon or by written confirmation from the rightsholder.

sensitivity.notes: Dictionary content may still need dialect and terminology review before use.

candidates.next_action: Inspect the downloadable PDF for a work-specific license statement and contact the press or rightsholder if the reuse grant remains unclear.

candidates.title: Anv-ke/Maasai

rights.rights_notes: The organization card lists CC BY 4.0 and an ethical warning against surveillance, discrimination, exploitation, or unethical profiling. Dataset access requires sharing contact information.
rights.restrictions: Human voice data requires privacy, consent, and downstream-use review before ingest.

sensitivity.notes: Speech data includes scripted and unscripted recordings and should not be ingested without documented handling constraints.

candidates.next_action: Request access, record the access conditions, confirm consent and privacy posture, and scope initial use to approved ASR evaluation or tightly controlled experiments.

candidates.title: Openverse audio discovery lane for Maasai and Maasai Mara queries

rights.rights_notes: Openverse documentation states that catalog items are under Creative Commons licenses or in the public domain, but also warns users to verify the license of a particular work before using it.
rights.restrictions: Use Openverse as a discovery surface only. Re-verify every selected item on the upstream source page.

sensitivity.notes: Aggregated search results may include community, classroom, or location-sensitive recordings.

candidates.next_action: Use query-level discovery for Maasai audio leads, then register each chosen upstream item separately with source-specific rights notes.

candidates.title: Maasai Camp Nature Sounds Africa

rights.rights_notes: Freesound marks the recording as Creative Commons 0.
rights.restrictions: Rights are clear for reuse, but the recording is ambient soundscape material rather than Maasai language data.

sensitivity.notes: Suitable only for non-linguistic exhibit or demo ambience after attribution and provenance are recorded.

candidates.next_action: If needed, review for demo ambience use only; do not classify as speech or translation training data.

candidates.title: Maasai Village Classroom

rights.rights_notes: Freesound marks the recording as Creative Commons 0.
rights.restrictions: Public-domain dedication does not remove the need for privacy, voice-consent, and contextual review for classroom recordings.

sensitivity.notes: Contains a classroom environment and should remain out of automated ingest until consent and community-appropriateness are documented.

candidates.next_action: Keep as a reference lead only unless speaker-consent and classroom-use appropriateness can be documented.

candidates.title: School Kids Counting Numbers

rights.rights_notes: Freesound marks the recording as Creative Commons 0.
rights.restrictions: The clip contains student voices and is described as children counting in English, so it should not be treated as Maasai speech data.

sensitivity.notes: Heightened privacy and consent review required because children are involved.

candidates.next_action: Keep out of ingest and training lanes; use only as a cautionary reference example for child-voice governance.

candidates.title: ngesa254/Learn-Maasai

rights.rights_notes: The repository has an Apache-2.0 license and advertises Maasai language learning functionality.
rights.restrictions: Review repository files and assets one-by-one before treating any embedded content as reusable training data.

sensitivity.notes: Useful as a mobile learning architecture lead, with content reuse gated by additional review.

candidates.next_action: Review UI and app architecture patterns, then approve or reject individual assets for reuse based on file-level rights checks.
